channel.title: CWE-200 on ZAP
channel.description: Recent content in CWE-200 on ZAP

item.title: Admin/management path observed
item.description:
Flags high-value endpoint patterns observed in traffic (admin panels, debug endpoints, consoles, and backup/config file paths).

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Android assetlinks.json observed
item.description:
Flags security-relevant well-known resources and metadata files when they appear in observed traffic.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: API docs endpoint observed
item.description:
Detects exposure of API documentation, specs, and interactive consoles observed in traffic.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Apple app-site-association observed
item.description:
Flags security-relevant well-known resources and metadata files when they appear in observed traffic.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: AWS Access Key ID pattern
item.description:
Flags secret-like tokens and exposed configuration values in observed HTML/JS/JSON responses. These are recon leads; validate sensitivity before reporting.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Cloud metadata IP referenced
item.description:
Detects internal hostnames/IPs and environment hints (staging/dev/local) disclosed in observed responses.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Content Security Policy Violations Reporting Enabled
Credit Card Number
item.description: Sensitive data is anything that should not be accessible to admin access, known as sensitive data. Sensitive data may include personally identifiable information (PII), such as Social Security numbers, financial information, or login credentials. Sensitive Data Exposure occurs when an organization unknowingly exposes sensitive data or when a security incident leads to the accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, or admin disclosure of, or access to sensitive data.

item.title: Debug/diagnostic path observed
item.description:
Flags high-value endpoint patterns observed in traffic (admin panels, debug endpoints, consoles, and backup/config file paths).

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Environment hints (dev/staging/test) in response
item.description:
Detects internal hostnames/IPs and environment hints (staging/dev/local) disclosed in observed responses.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Environment/config file observed
item.description:
Flags high-value endpoint patterns observed in traffic (admin panels, debug endpoints, consoles, and backup/config file paths).

item.title: GraphQL path observed
item.description:
Flags high-value endpoint patterns observed in traffic (admin panels, debug endpoints, consoles, and backup/config file paths).

item.title: HTML references .map files
item.description:
Detects source map references and common debug artifacts in observed HTML/JS responses. These are high-value recon leads for code disclosure and hidden endpoints.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Image Exposes Location or Privacy Data
item.description: The image was found to contain embedded location information, such as GPS coordinates, or another privacy exposure, such as camera serial number. Depending on the context of the image in the website, this information may expose private details of the users of a site. For example, a site that allows users to upload profile pictures taken in the home may expose the home’s address.

item.title: Information Disclosure - Amazon S3 Bucket URL
item.description: An Amazon S3 bucket URL was found in the HTTP response body.

item.title: Information Disclosure - Google API Key
item.description: A Google API Key was found in the HTTP response body.

item.title: Information Disclosure - IBAN Numbers
item.description: An IBAN number was discovered in the HTTP response body.

item.title: Information Disclosure - Private IP Address
item.description: A private IP such as 10.x.x.x, 172.x.x.x, 192.168.x.x or IPV6 fe00:: has been found in the HTTP response body. This information might be helpful for further attacks targeting internal systems.

item.title: Information Disclosure - Server Header
item.description: The web/application server is leaking version information via the ‘Server’ HTTP response header. Access to such information may facilitate attackers identifying other vulnerabilities your web/application server is subject to.

item.title: Information Disclosure - X-Powered-By Header
item.description: The web/application server is leaking information via one or more ‘X-Powered-By’ HTTP response headers. Access to such information may facilitate attackers identifying other frameworks/components your web application is reliant upon and the vulnerabilities such components may be subject to.

item.title: Internal IP address leaked in response
item.description:
Detects internal hostnames/IPs and environment hints (staging/dev/local) disclosed in observed responses.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: JavaScript includes sourceMappingURL
item.description:
Detects source map references and common debug artifacts in observed HTML/JS responses. These are high-value recon leads for code disclosure and hidden endpoints.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.description:

item.title: Server is running on Clacks - GNU Terry Pratchett
item.description: The web/application server is running over the Clacks network, some say it’s turtles/IP, some say it’s turtles all the way down the layer stack.

item.title: Social Security Number
item.description: Sensitive data is anything that should not be accessible to admin access, known as sensitive data. Sensitive data may include personally identifiable information (PII), such as Social Security numbers, financial information, or login credentials. Sensitive Data Exposure occurs when an organization unknowingly exposes sensitive data or when a security incident leads to the accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, or admin disclosure of, or access to sensitive data.
